Job Title: Practice Manager - CX and Conversational AI
Salary: Practice Manager CX and Conversational AI
Ad Text:
Our Why 

Datacom works with organisations and communities across Australia and New Zealand to make a difference in people’s lives and help organisations use the power of tech to innovate and grow.

About the Role (Your Why) 

At Datacom, we connect people and technology to solve challenges, create opportunities, and unlock new possibilities for the communities we serve. Our mission is simple: make a meaningful difference through the power of innovation. We partner with organisations and communities across Australia and New Zealand to improve lives and help businesses use technology to innovate, grow, and succeed.

As a Conversational AI & CX leader you will blend hands-on technical expertise with commercial acumen and the ability to engage deeply with customers at every level. You’ll design, build, showcase and optimise cutting-edge conversational solutions that inspire and deliver measurable business value, driving improved customer and employee experiences.

If you’re passionate about customers and obsessed with designing human-centric experiences that deliver ROI, this is your stage. You see the bigger picture, yet dive into the details, and you’re recognised as a thought leader and practitioner in the CX and EX space.

About the team

You’ll be part of our dynamic, fast-growing Conversational AI team across ANZ and Asia, collaborating with some of the most innovative and recognisable brands in Australia and New Zealand. In this pivotal role, you’ll help shape next-generation service experiences by applying Conversational AI and broader AI capabilities to transform customer engagement. Your ability to navigate ambiguity and guide teams through technical and organisational complexity will be essential to driving impactful outcomes across a broad range of industries.

We value flexibility – our offices are based in Adelaide, Brisbane, Canberra, Melbourne, Perth, Sydney, – and we support working from home too. We empower you and your team to balance collaboration and work-life harmony. If you’re an innovative thinker who thrives at the intersection of technology and customer outcomes, you’ll inspire clients with visionary ideas while delivering tangible, high-quality solutions. Your collaborative approach, technical credibility, and passion for CX/EX will make you a trusted advisor and a driver of change.

What You’ll Do

We work in an agile environment where no two days are the same – responding to evolving customer, technology, and business needs. You will:

Create compelling, customer-focused experiences powered by Conversational AI technologies and be comfortable presenting and discussing these with customers and prospects
Ideate, design, build, and deliver powerful demonstrations that showcase CAI capabilities to existing and prospective clients
Apply advanced conversational design principles to craft intuitive, human-centric interaction flows
Transition seamlessly between deep technical conversations with engineers and strategic discussions with business leaders
Collaborate with customers to shape their CX and EX visions – inspiring new ideas and provoking thoughts about the ‘art of the possible’
Identify automation opportunities in contact centre and retail environments that deliver ROI while enhancing frontline operations
Manage and optimise delivery of current solutions including intent management, conversational structure and integration with customers' digital environments
Work with customers to identify tuning and refining opportunities to increase bot containment and improve agent and customer satisfaction within risk tolerance and accessibility standards
Work effectively across stakeholders to translate ambitious customer visions into practical, scalable solutions
Apply change management methodologies and agile delivery techniques to ensure seamless adoption and lasting impact
Plan and integrate next‑generation technologies into the architectural strategy and build strategic relationships with software and hardware vendors to inform roadmaps and GTM approaches

What you’ll bring

Required Experience

Proven (3+ years) expertise in Conversational AI and CX strategy delivering measurable improvements
Strong understanding of automation in contact centre and retail environments
Ability to lead and contribute to project management and implementation activities
Exceptional stakeholder engagement and communication skills, able to navigate technical and business conversations
Experience with platforms such as Twilio, Cognigy, Genesys, and Microsoft
Proven leadership experience and excellent presentation and stakeholder management skills
Experience in solution and design services, scoping, configuration assistance and needs assessments

Nice to Have

Strong working knowledge of change management and agile delivery frameworks
Background in outsourcing or working with systems integrators
Experience with Prosci Change Management methodology
Familiarity with ITIL, application management and integration into core business systems
Understanding of key cloud vendors such as AWS, Azure, GCP and commercial awareness of vendor architectural strategies

Why join us here at Datacom?

Datacom is one of Australia and New Zealand’s largest suppliers of Information Technology professional services, while maintaining a dynamic, agile, small-business feel. It's our people that give Datacom its unique culture and energy.

At Datacom you'll be recognised and valued for your contributions. We're growing year on year and can provide stability, career opportunity and a collegial, agile, flat-structured environment that empowers people and promotes autonomy. We care about our people and provide a range of perks such as social events, chill-out spaces, remote working, flexi-hours and professional development courses to name a few. We operate at the leading edge of technology to help ANZ’s largest enterprise organisations explore possibilities and solve their greatest challenges, so you will never run out of interesting new challenges and opportunities.

If you would like to know more and this sounds like you. Please apply online today!

Location: ACT and Sydney

Contract Type: Long term

Work Arrangement: Hybrid

We Pay Weekly

A Federal Government agency is seeking a talented and dynamic Business Analyst to support a range of cyber‑related initiatives within its Information Technology Services (ITS) function. This role suits an experienced analyst who thrives in digital environments, is confident engaging with diverse stakeholders, and can translate complex business needs into clear, actionable solutions.

You will be client‑focused, comfortable working across all stages of the analysis lifecycle, and capable of developing high‑quality business cases, requirements documentation, and solution designs. This is an excellent opportunity to contribute to cyber uplift activities and modern digital service delivery.

Key Responsibilities

Deliver business analysis services to support implementation of the organisation's Cyber Security Strategy.
Collaborate with ITS and business project teams to gather and validate requirements, support testing, track issues, and ensure business outcomes are met.
Facilitate workshops and research sessions to identify pain points, design solutions, and uncover improvement opportunities.
Produce high‑quality artefacts such as business cases, submissions, user stories, journey maps, process maps, blueprints, personas, and functional specifications.
Contribute to solution design and ensure alignment with enterprise architecture, cyber security, information management and future business needs.

Essential Criteria

Minimum 8 years' experience as a senior Business Analyst on technical projects. Cyber security exposure is highly regarded.
Strong liaison, negotiation and problem‑solving skills.
Excellent written and verbal communication, with the ability to convey complex information clearly and engage effectively with stakeholders.
Demonstrated experience facilitating workshops with large stakeholder groups and synthesising input into actionable requirements.
Experience producing business analysis artefacts including business cases, user journeys, process maps, functional decomposition models, user stories and test cases.

How to Apply

Candidates must submit a one‑page pitch (maximum 5,000 characters, including spaces) addressing all essential criteria.

Divya Arora
Consultant

Wolfe Systems is growing its Dynamics capability and we are looking for a practical, customer-focused Dynamics 365 Business Central Consultant to join the team.

This role is ideal for someone who understands Business Central well, enjoys working closely with customers, and can help turn business requirements into practical ERP outcomes. You’ll be involved across discovery, scoping, implementation, migration planning, training and ongoing customer guidance.

This is not a pure developer role and it is not a purely strategic consulting role. We are looking for someone who can work hands-on with customers, understand how businesses operate, and help guide successful Business Central implementations from early planning through to adoption.


The Role

You will work with customers to understand their operational processes and requirements, then support the design and delivery of Business Central solutions that align to those needs.

You’ll be involved in implementation planning, solution configuration, migration readiness, process mapping, user training and general customer guidance. You will also work with internal stakeholders and technical resources where integrations, extensions or development inputs are required.

This is a delivery-focused role with strong customer interaction. It will suit someone who is comfortable asking the right questions, guiding customers through change and helping build structured, scalable ERP outcomes.




Key Responsibilities

Work with customers to understand business processes and operational requirements

Support pre-sales discovery, scoping and implementation planning

Configure and support delivery of Dynamics 365 Business Central solutions

Assist with process alignment across finance, purchasing, sales, inventory and warehousing

Support data migration readiness, planning and validation

Train end users and customer stakeholders in Business Central and related processes

Produce clear implementation, process and handover documentation

Work with internal developers or technical resources where required

Support customer adoption and continuous improvement after go-live

Travel to customer sites where required for workshops, implementation and training




What We’re Looking For

3+ years of relevant experience with Microsoft Dynamics 365 Business Central or closely related ERP environments

Strong functional understanding of Business Central, particularly across finance, purchasing, sales and warehousing

Experience working with customers in implementation, consulting or solution delivery environments

Ability to understand customer operations and translate them into practical system outcomes

Experience supporting data migration planning and implementation readiness

Strong communication skills and confidence in customer-facing discussions

Ability to deliver user training and support customer adoption

Strong organisational skills and ability to manage multiple delivery activities




Desirables

Experience with manufacturing workflows or manufacturing-related ERP processes

Experience with integrations, extensions or leading development inputs

Familiarity with Power Platform, automation or related Microsoft tooling

Microsoft Dynamics / Business Central certifications

Experience in mid-market customer environments

Experience working in a consulting, partner or managed services environment




What We Offer

Opportunity to help shape and grow Wolfe Systems’ Dynamics capability

Strong customer-facing role with real influence on delivery outcomes

Exposure to a range of customer environments and business processes

Supportive team environment with room to grow

Competitive salary based on experience

Opportunity to continue building Dynamics and broader Microsoft business application capability

Job Title: Domain Solution Architect – Mining, Oil & Gas
Ad Text:
DXC Technology (NYSE: DXC) is a leading enterprise technology and innovation partner delivering software, services, and solutions to global enterprises and public sector organizations — helping them harness AI to drive outcomes at a time of exponential change with speed. With deep expertise in Managed Infrastructure Services, Application Modernization, and Industry-Specific Software Solutions, DXC modernizes, secures, and operates some of the world’s most complex technology estates. Learn more on dxc.com

The Domain Solution Architect – Mining, Oil & Gas is a senior leadership role accountable for shaping, governing, and delivering analytics, AI enabled and digital transformation solutions primarily across Mining, Oil & Gas clients.

The role combines industry interlock leadership, solution architecture authority, presales accountability, and commercial ownership to drive sustainable revenue growth and ensure DXC delivers high‑quality, scalable, and operationally practical solutions within asset‑intensive and safety‑critical environments.

Key Responsibilities

Industry & Client Leadership

Act as Data & AI’s senior domain authority for Mining, Oil & Gas across Western Australia.

Build and maintain trusted advisor relationships with C‑suite, executive, and senior operational stakeholders including DXC’s internal client partners and sellers.

Provide industry insight and thought leadership to influence client strategy, investment decisions, and technology roadmaps.

Represent DXC in executive engagements, industry forums, and strategic client discussions.

Revenue Growth & Portfolio Ownership

Own and grow the Mining, Oil & Gas Data & AI’s portfolio, including pipeline, revenue, and total contract value.

Partner with Industry, Sales, and Account leaders to identify, qualify, and prioritise opportunities.

Lead strategic pursuits, account expansions, and large‑scale bids.

Improve win rates through disciplined opportunity qualification, value‑led solution framing, and strong solution governance.

Presales, Solution Architecture & Governance

Lead end‑to‑end solution shaping from discovery through proposal submission.

Design and govern solution architectures across data platforms, analytics, AI/ML, industrial IoT, cloud, and integration.

Ensure solutions are commercially viable, operationally practical, secure, and aligned to asset‑heavy environments.

Provide governance over solution assumptions, estimates, delivery models, risks, and dependencies.

Own or review technical responses for RFPs, RFTs, RFIs, and support executive presentations and workshops.

Delivery Alignment & Practice Leadership

Ensure solutions transition seamlessly into delivery and business outcomes are realised.

Partner with delivery leadership to ensure quality, margin protection, and client satisfaction.

Contribute to Mining, Oil & Gas‑specific offerings, accelerators, and go‑to‑market strategies.

Support capability development and mentoring across analytics and solution architecture teams.

KPIs & Success Measures

Revenue, pipeline, total contract value and margin attainment.

Bid win rate and opportunity conversion.

Solution quality measured by delivery predictability and reduced rework or escalations.

Client satisfaction, executive sponsorship, and repeat business.

Contribution to reusable assets, offerings, and practice capability uplift.

Margin protection and delivery governance effectiveness.

Maintain 40% utilisation on Data & AI project delivery.

Skills & Experience

Industry & Domain Leadership

15+ years’ experience leading enterprise data and digital initiatives across Mining, Oil & Gas and other asset intensive sectors operating models and safety‑critical environments.

Proven track record advising executives on data strategy, operating models, governance, compliance, and risk to drive measurable business outcomes.

Enterprise Data Strategy & Governance

Expertise in Data Governance & Security, Data Maturity Assessments, Strategic Lifecycle Management, and Enterprise Information/Data Modelling (Kimball, Inmon, Data Vault, Enterprise Information Model).

Hands‑on leadership with DMBOK/DAMA and TOGAF aligned practices; implementation of metadata management, lineage, quality, and master/reference data disciplines.

Architecture & Modern Platforms

End‑to‑end solution design across modern data platforms, including lakehouse and data fabric patterns; event‑driven and streaming architectures; microservices integration and DevOps enablement.

Advanced experience with Azure (Azure SQL, Azure Synapse Analytics, Azure Data Factory/ADF, Cosmos DB), AWS, Snowflake, Informatica and google data ecosystems.

Systems & Tools

Databricks (Delta Lake, ML pipelines) and Microsoft Fabric (e.g., OneLake, lakehouse, pipelines, semantic models).

Azure Synapse, Snowflake, Confluent (Kafka), MuleSoft, Salesforce, SAP, DBT, SSIS, ADF, PowerShell, Git, Jira/Confluence.

BI & Analytics: Power BI, Tableau, SSRS; advanced Python/R analytics workflows and enablement of data science platforms.

Data Engineering & Integration

Design and leadership of robust ingestion, transformation, and inter‑system interoperability across ERP/CRM and OT/IT boundaries.

Implementation of reusable data migration engines and reconciliation/exception reporting at scale.

Presales, Commercials & Delivery Governance

Executive presales leadership (discovery, solution options, estimation, assumption & risk registers), RFP/RFT authorship, solution governance, and handover into delivery with clear NFRs and transition plans.

Proven margin protection through architecture guardrails, risk mitigation, quality gates, and measurable delivery outcomes.

Leadership & Practice Building

Practice uplift (capability frameworks, accelerators, reusable IP, and cross‑functional collaboration with product, security, finance, and operations.

At DXC Technology, we believe strong connections and community are key to our success. Our work model prioritizes in-person collaboration while offering flexibility to support wellbeing, productivity, individual work styles, and life circumstances. We’re committed to fostering an inclusive environment where everyone can thrive.

These emails may request recipients to provide personal information or to make payments as part of their illegitimate recruiting process. DXC does not make offers of employment via social media networks and DXC never asks for any money or payments from applicants at any point in the recruitment process, nor ask a job seeker to purchase IT or other equipment on our behalf. More information on employment scams is available here.

About ElectraNet

ElectraNet builds, owns, operates and maintains the high voltage electricity transmission network which delivers the energy South Australians rely on to power our homes, businesses and communities. Our people describe our culture as supportive, inclusive and community‑oriented, where we genuinely want each other to succeed.

As an industry leader, we are at the forefront of South Australia's clean energy transition and are committed to strengthening how work flows across the organisation to support sustainable performance.

Employee Benefits

ElectraNet is committed to empowering our people and recognising their contribution with a competitive remuneration package of $146,000 + 12% superannuation + bonus (up to 15% of package). In addition, we are proud to offer:

5 weeks annual leave per year
15 days personal/carers leave per year (plus an additional 5 days on first day upon starting with the organisation)
16 weeks paid parental leave
Additional paid volunteering and office closure leave
Flexibility to utilise public holiday leave entitlements for other cultural celebrations
Employee Assistance Program for your personal and professional wellbeing

Visit our careers page to read more about our employee benefits.

The Opportunity

Reporting to the Head of Business Improvement, the Process Excellence Lead helps ElectraNet improve how work gets done by designing simpler, more efficient and customer‑focused end‑to‑end processes.

Working closely with leaders and cross‑functional teams, the role applies lean thinking, service design and data‑driven analysis to understand current‑state processes, identify inefficiencies and design practical, sustainable improvements.

This is a hands‑on process design role. The Process Excellence Lead focuses on doing the deep process work — translating business needs into tangible artefacts and supporting teams to implement improved ways of working.

Key aspects of the role include:

Mapping complex end‑to‑end processes using customer journey mapping, service blueprinting and value stream mapping
Designing future‑state processes that improve flow, reduce waste and enhance customer and employee experience
Analysing cycle times, hand‑offs, pain points and inefficiencies using qualitative and quantitative data
Translating insights into clear artefacts such as journey maps, service blueprints, process documentation and requirements
Facilitating workshops and working sessions to align stakeholders and co‑design solutions
Measuring success through improved flow, simplified processes and measurable operational outcomes
About You

This role suits a process specialist who enjoys getting into the detail and improving how work actually gets done.

You may come from a background such as business analysis, process excellence, lean operations, service design or operational transformation, and have demonstrated experience designing and improving complex processes across multiple stakeholder groups.

We're looking for someone who:

Has strong experience in customer journey mapping and /or service blueprinting, including personas and multiple perspectives (e.g. customer, employee, technology, leaders)
Is comfortable working with complex, cross‑functional processes and identifying waste, hand‑offs and bottlenecks
Applies lean principles pragmatically to improve flow and efficiency
Is curious, patient and resilient, and consistently challenges legacy ways of working to rethink processes
Can facilitate sessions and influence stakeholders to think differently about processes
Enjoys translating ambiguity into clear, usable artefacts
How To Apply

Please apply online via our careers page before 22 April 2026. Interested applicants are encouraged to apply as soon as possible, as interviews and appointments may occur prior to the closing date. Applicants can also register for job alerts via our careers page. Position Descriptions can be obtained by clicking here.

Successful applicant will be required to undergo background checks. These checks may include global criminal history checks, reference checks, and validation of qualifications, employment history, Australian work rights, and any additional checks depending on the role.

ElectraNet is committed to creating a diverse and inclusive workplace. We welcome applications from people of all backgrounds, identities, and lived experiences—including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, people with disabilities, neurodivergent individuals, and those from culturally and linguistically diverse communities.

We are proud to be a Work180 Endorsed Employer. You can read more about our key benefits, including flexible work options, leave and other supports ﻿on our website.

Job Title: Senior Delivery Manager (Senior Manager Consulting level)
Ad Text:
Are you ready to unlock your true potential?
Who are we? #Tranzformd

Tranzformd is a management consulting and program delivery company. We leverage our extensive network of highly motivated, skilled and experienced professionals to deliver optimal results for enterprise business.
Through our bespoke, human-centric engagement style, Tranzformd partners with ambitious organisations to achieve transformational goals.
We are passionate about transforming the consultants of today into the consultants of the future. We empower our people to do their best work through exceptional training, development and mentoring programs, and together we produce world-class outputs.
As a consultancy, we work on diverse projects that provide continuous learning, dynamic client interaction, and the opportunity to impact businesses of all sizes and sectors.

Position Purpose
We are seeking a Senior Manager-level Consultant to work with one of our key clients on a major technology uplift program, playing a critical role in ensuring alignment across business, technology, and design.

This role requires someone who has worked on large, complex transformation initiatives and knows how to bring structure, clarity, and momentum across multiple moving parts.

You will act as the connector across the program, working closely with senior stakeholders while embedding a strong UX/CX lens to ensure solutions are both effective and user-centric.

This is an initial 6-month FTC, based in Melbourne and needing 3 days in the office.

The opportunity #yourrole
By joining Tranzformd, you will work with ASX listed companies on transformation strategies, initiatives and programs of work. This may include, but is not limited to, the following:

Key Responsibilities

Act as the link across business, technology, and design teams, ensuring alignment and progress across the program
Lead senior stakeholder engagement, building credibility and trust across the client organisation
Bring a customer and user-first mindset, shaping outcomes through UX/CX principles and service design thinking
Support delivery of a large-scale technology transformation, ensuring initiatives stay on track and aligned to business outcomes
Facilitate workshops and decision-making forums to drive clarity and momentum
Translate complex requirements into clear, actionable direction for delivery teams
Identify and navigate risks, dependencies, and competing priorities across the program
Work closely with cross-functional teams including product, engineering, and design
Be the key liaison in building the Tranzformd team onsite and ensuring delivery standards are met.

What We're Looking For

Experience operating at Senior Manager / Lead Consultant level within consulting or large transformation environments
Strong track record delivering or supporting enterprise-wide transformation initiatives (technology, digital, or operating model)
Ability to connect the dots across complex programs, bringing structure without slowing delivery
Proven experience managing and influencing senior stakeholders
Exposure to UX/CX, service design, or customer-led transformation
Comfortable operating across strategy through to execution
Good at leading onsite consulting teams
Strong facilitation, communication, and problem-solving skills
A pragmatic, hands-on consultant who can adapt quickly and add value immediately

Who are you joining? The #Tranzformd community

#Tranzformdawards2024 & 2025

6th in the Deloitte Tech Fast 50 2024, and 39th in 2025
28th in the The Australian Financial Review hashtag#AFR Top 100 Fast starters 2024

Our vision:
To bring the best, most experienced talent on the market and pair it with a relentless commitment to excellence, quality, and risk management, to consistently achieve exceptional outcomes that exceed expectations.

Varied and Impactful Projects: At #Tranzformd you'll work on diverse, high-impact projects across multiple industries.
* Accelerated Professional Development: We're committed to helping you advance your career, with ongoing training and mentorship from our team of experts and community.
* Collaborative & Supportive Environment: You'll join a team that values collaboration and knowledge-sharing. We learn together, solve problems, and support each other to deliver the best results for our clients.
* Work-Life Balance & Flexibility: We understand work-life balance matters. With flexible work options, you can design a schedule that works for you while delivering top-tier results for our clients.
* Competitive Compensation & Benefits: Enjoy a competitive salary, comprehensive benefits, and a rewarding career path with opportunities for advancement.

If you're ready to make a difference in the world of transformation and work on exciting, diverse projects, join us at #Tranzformd, a consultancy that values innovation, growth, and teamwork.

We are currently building a high-performing tender and strategy team to support a major rail infrastructure joint venture on a significant upcoming bid within Victoria.


This is a rare opportunity to contribute to a large-scale, city-shaping transport project, working across strategy, technology, and delivery to help develop a compelling and competitive tender submission.
We are seeking multiple senior professionals with expertise across a range of domains to play a key role in shaping the bid.

Key Responsibilities
Support tender strategy and solution development across technology and operational domains
Conduct current-state analysis and identify risks, gaps, and improvement opportunities
Contribute to the future-state technology roadmap (8–11 years)
Engage with stakeholders across IT and Operational Technology (OT) environments
Develop high-quality bid content, artefacts, and supporting documentation
Provide input into costing, implementation planning, and transition approach
About You
15+ years’ experience as a Senior Business Analyst / Consultant in complex environments
Proven experience working on large-scale tenders or bids
Strong ability to operate in fast-paced, ambiguous environments
Excellent stakeholder engagement and written communication skills
Melbourne-based and available to start immediately

Applications & Data

Cybersecurity & Compliance
Operational Technology (OT)
Highly Regarded

What’s on Offer
Work on a high-profile rail infrastructure bid

Opportunity to influence both bid success and long-term delivery outcomes
Apply Now If you have strong tender experience and domain expertise within infrastructure or transport, we’d love to hear from you. Apply now or reach out for a confidential discussion.

A rare opportunity to lead strategy and partnerships at scale in a future focused environment.

Somerset College are seeking an exceptional and forward-thinking leader to take on the role of Head of Strategy and Partnerships, a pivotal position shaping the future direction of the College. Reporting to the Headmaster and working closely with the College Leadership Team, this role sits at the centre of strategy, innovation and collaboration. It offers a rare opportunity to influence whole-of-College priorities, build meaningful partnerships, and lead initiatives that will have a lasting impact on our people, programs and global engagement.

The Opportunity

This leadership role is responsible for driving key strategic initiatives and enabling their successful delivery across Somerset College. In its early phase, the role will focus on establishing a new Institute, advancing global partnerships and programs, and embedding a consistent approach to project and change management. You will work across diverse portfolios, balancing competing priorities while ensuring strong governance, clear communication, and alignment to the College’s strategic plan. Success in this role will see the development of systems, partnerships and initiatives that position the College as a leader in education, both locally and globally.

✅ KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Institute Development and Strategy

Establish and lead the College’s new Institute, aligning with strategic priorities

Design and implement the structure, focus areas, and measures of success

Position the College as a leader in education through partnerships and key initiatives

Build relationships with universities, industry, and education networks

Support innovation and research in areas such as character education, AI, and global programs

Global Programs and Partnerships

Lead a cohesive global programs strategy across the College

Expand opportunities for exchanges, study abroad, and international collaboration

Strengthen and grow key global relationships

Project and Change Leadership

Establish a consistent project management framework

Oversee delivery of strategic projects aligned to the College plan

Lead change initiatives, including stakeholder engagement and communication

Identify and implement improvements to systems, processes, and ways of working

Reporting & Communication

Provide clear reporting and insights to the Headmaster and Leadership Team

Prepare high-quality briefings and presentations

Ensure consistent communication of strategic priorities across the College

✅ WE OFFER
Competitive Remuneration – be rewarded with competitive pay and entitlements
Fantastic Perks – enjoy onsite gym, barista, catering services and parking access
Growth Opportunity – showcase and develop skills collaborating with a talented team
Community Culture – join a learning culture enriched by values of respect and integrity
Ideal Gold Coast Location – experience our vibrant city with natural beauty, pristine coastline and breathtaking wilderness

✅ ABOUT US
Somerset College is one of Australia's leading co-educational independent schools, educating over 1,700 students from Pre-Prep to Year 12. Nestled in the heart of the Gold Coast on a stunning 22-hectare campus in Mudgeeraba, we are proud of our global outlook, academic excellence, and commitment to student success.

✅ ABOUT YOU
You are a strategic, collaborative leader who thrives in a complex and evolving environment. You will bring:

Proven leadership experience in strategy, projects, or change

Strong ability to build partnerships across education, industry, and global networks

Excellent influencing, communication, and stakeholder engagement skills

Experience leading initiatives across multiple teams and priorities

Strong organisational, analytical, and problem-solving capability

Ability to deliver outcomes in a dynamic environment

A commitment to innovation, continuous improvement, and the College’s values

Join the College and play a defining role in shaping its future direction and impact.

APPLY NOW
📌 To apply, please submit:
✅Your resume (max. 5 pages), including 2 professional referees
✅A cover letter (max. 3 pages) outlining your interest in the role and address what you bring to the position against the suitability criteria detailed in the Position Prospectus and position description.

🌏 Learn more about Somerset College and find a Position Prospectus and Position Description at www.somerset.qld.edu.au

About nxzen

nxzen is a global technology, consulting, and engineering partner supporting the energy, utilities, and national infrastructure sectors. We help organisations optimise performance, digitise operations, and build safer, smarter, and more resilient systems by combining deep domain expertise with innovative technology.

Our work spans the full infrastructure lifecycle, partnering with clients to deliver practical, outcome-driven solutions across operations, transformation, digital platforms, and engineering. Through our specialist teams and Centres of Excellence, we support the delivery of future-ready infrastructure that enhances efficiency, resilience, and long-term sustainability.

About the ROLE: Head of Data & AI

Location: Sydney

Permanent Full-time Role

The Head of Data & AI (Artificial Intelligence) will shape, build, and enable the delivery of nxzen’s Data and Artificial Intelligence proposition, with a primary focus on leveraging AI for Asset Intelligence and Operational Excellence. The role targets Utilities in Australia generally, with a particular emphasis on Australian Electricity Distribution Networks.

This role reports to the Digital CoE (Centre of Excellence) Head and collaborates closely with colleagues in the wider nxzen group. Including but not limited to other CoE teams, Client Partners, other group companies, etc. The role is both market-facing and works internally to build our capabilities in applying Artificial Intelligence to drive smarter asset management, predictive analytics, Operational Excellence, and performance improvement across utilities in Australia, with a particular focus on electricity distribution networks.

Key responsibilities for the role include: 

Strategy & Growth

Define and execute the Data & AI (Artificial Intelligence) strategy for Asset Intelligence and Operational Excellence, aligned to nxzen's business objectives and the needs of Australian utility clients.

Identify and prioritise opportunities to expand nxzen's footprint across Australian Electricity Distribution Networks and the broader utilities sector.

Work with Client Partners, clients, and NNNCo to develop go-to-market solutions and bids that combine AI-driven analytics, IoT, and asset performance improvement.

Develop compelling value propositions that demonstrate how Artificial Intelligence can enhance asset intelligence, operational efficiency, and network resilience for electricity distribution businesses.

Client & Stakeholder Engagement

Act as a senior point of contact for key utility clients, particularly Australian Electricity Distribution Networks, building trusted relationships with operations, digital, and engineering leaders.

Support Client Partners and Delivery Directors in shaping client transformation journeys that leverage AI for asset intelligence and operational excellence outcomes.

Represent nxzen externally at industry events, contributing to thought leadership and brand positioning in the Australian utilities and energy sector.

Service & Solution Development

Shape the Data & AI product and service roadmap, ensuring solutions are technically robust, scalable, and commercially competitive for the Australian electricity distribution market.

Drive the development of AI-powered solutions for asset health monitoring, predictive maintenance, risk assessment, network optimisation, and operational decision-making.

Maintain awareness of emerging technologies and trends (AI/ML, generative AI, IoT, 5G, edge computing, OT security, digital twins) and translate these into practical solutions for utility clients.

Programme Delivery Oversight

Oversee successful delivery of Data & AI programmes for utility clients, ensuring outcomes are met on quality, cost, and schedule.

Implement strong governance, assurance, and performance metrics across projects, with particular attention to the regulatory and compliance environment of Australian electricity distribution.

Ensure operational compliance with security, safety, and regulatory requirements applicable to Australian utility networks.

About YOU

Essential Knowledge

Extensive experience in Data & Artificial Intelligence, with a demonstrated ability to apply AI/ML techniques to asset intelligence and operational excellence in utility or infrastructure environments.

Deep understanding of Australian Electricity Distribution Networks, including regulatory frameworks (AER), network management challenges, and asset lifecycle management.

Proven ability to operate across IT, OT, and ET environments, integrating diverse systems to drive digital transformation and enhance asset performance using AI.

Hands-on experience managing, analysing, and deriving insights from IoT devices, OT systems, digital twins, simulation platforms, and asset health models (e.g., NARMS, CNAIM).

Demonstrated track record of leveraging AI and integrated data to inform asset performance improvement, risk assessment, and strategic decision-making.

Extensive experience applying IEC CIM, ISO 14224, and Ops 4.0 frameworks to support AI-driven asset intelligence and operational excellence initiatives.

Skilled in collaborating with cross-functional teams and stakeholders to design and deliver technically robust, scalable, and commercially viable AI-powered asset intelligence solutions.

Bachelor’s degree in engineering, Computer Science, Data Science, Information Technology, or a related discipline.

Desirable Experience

Experience selling or delivering AI and data solutions to Australian electricity distribution businesses (e.g., Ausgrid, Endeavour Energy, Essential Energy, AusNet, Powercor, SA Power Networks, Energex, Ergon Energy, Western Power, or similar).

Familiarity with Australian energy market structures, the National Electricity Rules, and the regulatory investment test (RIT-D) processes.

Experience with AI/ML platforms and tools commonly used in utility asset management and operational analytics.

Track record of building and leading high-performing teams in a consulting or professional services environment.

Professional certifications in Data & AI, IoT, or Analytics (e.g., Certified Data Scientist, AI/ML certifications, or Analytics Professional certifications).

ISO 55000 / Asset Management certification.

IEC CIM or related utility data modelling standards.

OT / Cybersecurity certifications (e.g., CISSP, CISA, or relevant OT security training).

 How to APPLY

To apply, please submit a copy of your updated resume. Screening will be conducted as we receive applications, and ONLY shortlisted applicants will be contacted.

For more information or to have a confidential discussion, please contact our People & Culture Manager, Shreya Pahwa on shreya.pahwa@nxzen.com.

Job Title: Business Analysis Domain Lead- Customer Transition
Ad Text:
About the role – Full-time, 12-month fixed term contract

We have an exciting opportunity for a Business Analysis Domain Lead to join Transformation Program & Delivery.  

As a leader embedded within a program, you will "dual-hat" as both an expert business analysis practitioner and a people leader, driving BA capability uplift and shaping the culture of our delivery teams as we create a new bank.

Our team of Business Analysts are at the forefront of shaping the creation of a new bank by working on exciting and complex projects.

The Business Analysis Domain Lead is a pivotal role designed to drive high-impact change by partnering with Program Leadership to deliver business value with quality, pace, and flow. You will lead a team of Business Analysts, ensuring they are aligned to value outcomes while personally managing end-to-end requirements for critical initiatives. This role is at the heart of our "People First" culture, focusing on coaching, engagement, and the strategic optimization of BA resources to meet delivery demand.

Key responsibilities include:  

·        Lead end-to-end business analysis across the project lifecycle – Elicit, analyse and document requirements; process and related artefacts; user stories, use cases and storyboards; and support design, build, test and deployment to ensure solutions truly meet business needs. Champion legal and compliance obligations requirements applicable, where required engage with business owner to ensure compliance is met. 

·        Migration & Transformation Excellence: Direct the analysis of source-to-target data and process gaps. You will be responsible for identifying and documenting transitional requirements to ensure a seamless "Cutover" from legacy systems to our new banking platform.

·        People Leadership & Engagement – Actively develop direct reports through coaching and performance management. You will drive employee engagement scores by fostering a supportive BA community and role-modelling our bank’s values.

Strategic Delivery & Value Alignment – Drive the delivery of critical initiatives according to program roadmaps. Partner with Program Owners and Tech Leads to ensure BA activities maximise customer value and align with strategic objectives.

Stakeholder & Risk Management – Strengthen alignment between Chapter and Program stakeholders. Own and manage risk within appetite, ensuring controls support business objectives and coaching teams to uplift risk behaviours.

Capacity & Resource Optimisation – Take ownership of accurate resource forecasting across the program. Optimise resource allocation and ensure efficient use of team capability to meet fluctuating delivery demands.

Capability Uplift & Continuous Improvement – Embed BA chapter standards and tooling. Lead the adoption of new ways of working and drive value-driven improvements that raise overall program performance and professional development standards.

 About you 

This is more than a traditional lead role; it is a leadership position that requires a strategic mindset and a passion for developing people. You are a "People Leader" first, dedicated to shaping a high-performing culture while maintaining the technical excellence required to navigate complex banking transformations.


 To be successful in the role, you must have the following qualifications or skills:

Proven Leadership Experience – Demonstrated ability to lead, recruit, and mentor BA teams, with a track record of uplifting capabilities and fostering high engagement.

Proven Transformation Experience: A track record of success in large-scale transformation programs, specifically in mapping complex data/process gaps and managing the nuances of transitional states.

Expert Analytical Skills (10+ years) – A seasoned BA with 8+ experience in all facets of business analysis.  Deep experience in Finance or complex IT environments, with mastery of requirements elicitation and delivery across both Agile and Waterfall methodologies.

Strategic Stakeholder Management – Exceptional ability to bridge business and technology, building trusted relationships with Senior Leaders and Program Owners.

Risk & Financial Acumen – Experience embedding risk culture and managing resource forecasts to optimise team utilisation within a program.

Growth Mindset – A proactive approach to professional development and a "Resourceful & Optimising" mindset.

Financial services industry experience

Customer centric product development 

Process analysis and documentation 

Data management/mapping; Technical interface specification development 

Adaptable and capable in dealing with complexities and ambiguity in a constantly evolving environment. 

Experience managing multiple activities and issues across numerous projects in a high paced environment. 

Desirable skills for the role:

Customer centric product development in Banking eco-systems and integration

Process analysis and documentation 

Technical interface specification development 

Proficiency in SQL for data querying, analysis, and reporting 

Experience in digital API source-to-target mapping for data integration and transformation.  

Experience working within a customer-focused team in the Payments, Cards and Fraud environment. 

Qualified Professional – Degree qualified with ECBA (or higher) IIBA, Lean Six Sigma certification and proficiency in JIRA, Confluence, and Visio.

Strong Agile methodology certifications/experience (Scrum, SAFe, etc) and proactive promoter of lean, agile best practices  

 Location

Whilst our primary office locations are across Adelaide, Brisbane and Toowoomba, we are pleased to be able to consider applications from employees within Australia for this role. We also encourage you to put thought into how the work may be undertaken given your location and what support you may require.

Why you’ll love working with us: 

People First Bank – our new brand – says exactly what we’re all about: people. We're committed to supporting and growing our team and creating an outstanding work environment.

You’ll have access to financial and lifestyle benefits to support your success and individuality:

Flexibility and hybrid working arrangements.

Enhance your salary with discounts on banking, health insurance, and shopping across Australia.

Advance your career with training, study support, and project opportunities.

Competitive leave benefits that empower employees to take time for themselves and loved ones.

Wellbeing support with our Employee Assistance Program, wellbeing leave, mental health app, and mental health ambassadors’ program.

Make a difference with paid volunteering, Workplace Giving, and diverse community initiatives.

Be recognised for your contributions through our peer-driven recognition program.

 A bit about People First Bank:  

In March 2023, People’s Choice and Heritage Bank merged to form Heritage and People’s Choice Limited - Australia's leading mutual bank.  We are now on an exciting journey to bring together our products, services, and technology under a single brand - People First Bank.

We have over 700,000 members and 1,900 employees, with dual head offices in Adelaide and Toowoomba, an Australian-based contract centre and branches across five states. 

With a vision ‘To deliver positive change through banking’, People First Bank serves members and contributes to the community, while offering an inclusive, flexible work environment. We’re big enough to make an impact; but agile enough to influence and do interesting work.

Apply online now:

If this sounds like you, this could be the next step in your career. Click 'Apply' now and submit your application, including a cover letter and current resume by COB Friday 3rd April 2026.

We are committed to diversity and inclusion and support candidate requests for adjustments to enable everyone to equitably participate in our selection process.

Enterprise wide transformation in leading big 4 bank
6 month initial day rate contract
$700-800 per day

About the Role
An opportunity has arisen for an experienced PMO Analyst to join a large-scale regulatory remediation program within a leading Australian bank. This program forms part of a broader regulatory commitment and enforceable undertaking and is focused on strengthening how sensitive customer situations are managed, including hardship, bereavement, collections and complaints.

The program is now entering a critical phase with a newly established leadership team and significant investment to stabilise and accelerate delivery. The PMO Analyst will play a key role in supporting program governance, reporting and delivery coordination across multiple work streams, helping to bring structure, visibility and control to a complex, enterprise-wide initiative.

The program consists of several key streams including Bereavement, Deceased Estates, Hardship, Collections, Complaints Management, Customer Event Management and Customer Remediation, all contributing to a high-profile transformation running through to 2027.

Key Responsibilities

Support PMO capability across a complex regulatory remediation program within retail banking
Coordinate governance, reporting and delivery activities across multiple program work streams
Consolidate RAID logs, status reports and delivery updates into a single program-level view
Track interdependencies, milestones and critical paths across streams
Support SteerCo, governance forums and regulatory reporting requirements
Maintain consistency in PMO standards, templates and reporting cadence
Provide oversight across risks, issues, dependencies and delivery progress
Support program financials, resourcing and forecasting activities
Work closely with stream leads, project managers and senior stakeholders to enable effective delivery

About You

Proven experience operating as a PMO Analyst within large-scale, multi-stream programs
Background in banking or financial services, ideally within regulatory or remediation environments
Strong understanding of governance, reporting, risk and issue management
Experience consolidating multiple work streams into a clear program-level view
High attention to detail with the ability to manage complexity and competing priorities
Strong stakeholder management and communication skills
Experience supporting program uplift or recovery environments will be highly regarded
This is a high-impact opportunity to play a key role in stabilising and delivering a critical regulatory program within a leading banking organisation.

Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Peoples are encouraged to apply.

To apply please click apply or call Kylie Wei on 03 8628 2163 for a confidential discussion.

Job Title: Senior Technical Business Analyst - Data and Analytics
Ad Text:
Company description:



About Capgemini:

Capgemini is a diverse collective of more than 350,000 strategic and technological experts based across more than 50 countries, partnering with world-renowned clients to transform and manage their businesses.

We are dedicated to leveraging cloud, data, AI, connectivity, software, digital engineering, and platforms to address the entire breadth of their business needs. This passion drives a powerful commitment - to unlock the true value of technology. ​

Our business in Australia and New Zealand has over 3,000 team members devoted to helping clients get the future they want.

Now is the time to join our rapidly growing team who are at the forefront of finding new ways technology can help us reimagine what's possible, collecting unique career experiences with global brands and game-changing tech projects.



Job description:

Job Description

About the Service Line:

Capgemini Global Insights & Data business line is a market leader in Data Engineering, Cloud Data Platforms, Data Science, and AI and Advanced Analytics across all sectors including financial services, public sector, consumer products, telecommunication & energy resources. Our offerings include end-2-end data integration to cloud platforms, complete suite of data engineering capabilities and a category of sector-based advanced analytics and AI driven solutions.

This is a unique opportunity within the Insights & Data (I&D) Global Business Line (GBL) to help grow the data, AI and Data Science engagements across all sectors in Australia and New Zealand. You will help shape and deliver complex projects and programs of work for our clients. You will understand our client's need to bring maximum value, impact, and innovation to the market as you oversee the delivery of client engagements for data and AI initiatives.



Let's talk about the roles and responsibilities:

The technical business analyst will work closely with business and technical stakeholders to elicit, analyse, validate and document business and technical requirements across data and analytics initiatives. This includes requirements related to data ingestion, transformation, modelling, reporting and analytics use cases.
Translate business needs into clear, structured artefacts such as requirements specifications, user stories, acceptance criteria, process models, data mappings and solution designs.
Act as a key bridge between business stakeholders and delivery teams including data engineers, analytics engineers, architects and reporting specialists.
Facilitate and lead workshops, interviews and design sessions to drive shared understanding, clarify scope, and support solution alignment.
Produce high‑quality written documentation and presentations suitable for both technical and non‑technical audiences.
Build strong, trusted relationships with stakeholders through effective communication, expectation management and proactive engagement.
Work within Agile and/or hybrid delivery environments, participating in ceremonies such as backlog refinement, sprint planning and showcases.
Experience and Qualifications:
Demonstrated experience as a Business Analyst / Technical Business Analyst, with a solid foundation in core business analysis practices and techniques.
Strong stakeholder engagement skills, with the ability to communicate effectively across business and technical audiences.
Proven experience facilitating workshops, requirement‑gathering sessions and collaborative design activities.
Experience delivering Data and Analytics projects, including data platforms, reporting, dashboards or analytics solutions.
Understanding of SQL and relational data concepts, with the ability to work with data engineers and analysts on data‑driven requirements.
Familiarity with data reporting and visualisation tools and concepts (e.g. enterprise reporting, dashboards, KPIs).
Strongly preferred: Exposure to modern cloud data platform technologies (e.g. Azure, AWS, GCP, lakehouse or data warehouse platforms).
Experience working in Agile delivery environments (e.g. Scrum, Kanban) is desirable.
What You'll Love About Working Here:
Exposure to transformational programs in AI & Data portfolio.
Career growth through learning platforms, certifications, and global mobility.
Inclusive culture backed by our "Inclusive Future for All" commitment.
Competitive total rewards and recognition programs.

As a Lead Data Scientist, you'll lead a growing data science team and play a pivotal role in shaping and delivering data-driven solutions across stakeholders. Your primary responsibilities include leading and mentoring your team, designing and implementing advanced machine learning and statistical models, translating complex business problems into scalable data products, and leading the end-to-end lifecycle from discovery to production. You will lead by example with modern data science techniques and tooling, providing clear technical direction and coaching to team members. 

We’re looking for a collaborative team player who thrives in a dynamic environment, embraces the challenge of legacy systems, remains composed under pressure, and is driven to deliver continuous improvement and strategic impact.  This role provides a unique opportunity to connect directly with our stakeholders.  You should be a versatile data leader who can both drive hands-on delivery and lead the development of new data initiatives—balancing strategic oversight with practical execution. 

Job Description


On a typical day you will: 

Lead, mentor and motivate a data science team (up to10 people) and set the technical direction and standards 
Collaborate within a cross-functional digital team, including Product Management, Engineering and Design. 
Bridge the gap between business problems and data science solutions, turning opportunities into impactful, production-ready models. 
Utilise strong analytical and modelling skills to design, build, and validate machine learning solutions, particularly tree-based methods (e.g. XGBoost, GBM, LightGBM), neural networks, and transformer-based approaches. 
Define and lead the end-to-end lifecycle of data products in an agile environment, from problem framing and experimentation through to deployment and monitoring. 
Conduct and document detailed analysis, including business case development, to prioritise use cases and clearly communicate trade-offs and expected value to stakeholders. 
Work closely with data and platform engineers on ETL pipelines, containerised solutions (e.g. Docker, Kubernetes), and cloud-based environments (e.g. GCP, AWS, Azure), with exposure to modern data platforms such as Snowflake and Databricks. 
Collaborate with cross-functional Agile teams (Engineering, Product, Design, QA) in remote and on-site settings, applying appropriate governance and engagement models to data projects. 
Provide BAU support for existing models and data products, while actively managing demand, prioritisation, and stakeholder expectations to create space for strategic, high-impact data science initiatives. 

Qualifications


Proven end-to-end Data Science experience (6+ years), including recent experience operating at Senior/Lead level 
Proven ability to lead, mentor and motivate a data science team (typically 3–10 people) and to set technical direction and standards 
Strong, hands-on expertise with modern machine learning models and algorithms, particularly tree-based methods (e.g. XGBoost, GBM, LightGBM), statistical models, and neural networks 
Demonstrated experience working with LLMs, transformers and embeddings in practical, production-oriented use cases 
Solid understanding of the end-to-end lifecycle of data products, from problem framing and model development through to productisation, pipelining and BAU support 
Proficiency in Python and a strong command of SQL for data exploration, feature engineering and model implementation 
Experience working with cloud platforms (e.g. GCP, AWS or Azure) and familiarity with modern data ecosystems such as Snowflake and/or Databricks (or ability to upskill quickly) 
Exposure to containerised solutions (e.g. Docker) and collaborating with engineers on production deployment and MLOps practices 
Strong stakeholder management and communication skills, with the ability to translate business problems into data solutions and tell a compelling story with insights 
Experience contributing to or leading governance and engagement models (e.g. steering committees, RACI/RASCI frameworks, working groups) across multiple stakeholders 
Demonstrated capability in prioritisation and managing tradeoffs in a resourceconstrained or scaleup environment, including pushing back on lowvalue work 
Experience developing or contributing to business cases for data and AI initiatives, including articulating value, risks and assumptions

Initial focus will be on working through the upcoming IRAP audit, maturing their ISMS and taking the broader team on a journey of security uplift.

After this initial phase, there is a possibility of this team growing and you being responsible for building this team yourself.

If you have prior experience in similar roles where you have implemented application security or DevSecOps principals in a government business, this role would be an ideal fit. Similarly, if you have experience leading a Cyber GRC function in a government business this is also well aligned.

Culture / Benefits

Hybrid - remote friendly team with expectation of time in office low
A tight knit and growing team in a well funded government department
Mature environment with a leader who is a direct communicator


Duties

Control mapping and uplift to ISM and PSPF standards
Work with a range of business stakeholders to educate and implement controls
Build upon their existing function to implement security into the SDLC
Define and advise DevSecOps style principals
Deliver hands-on to build out GRC framework


Skills and experience

Previous experience as a senior or lead in a GRC function
Strong knowledge and experience across various government frameworks - ISM, PSPF etc
Experience working through an IRAP audit
Knowledge around security software development principals
Strong stakeholder engagement skills and experience
Previous experience implementing a DevSecOps or AppSec program (nice to have)
Collaborative personality type to work with various stakeholders
Industry certifications are highly regarded

From Fivetran’s founding until now, our mission has remained the same: to make access to data as simple and reliable as electricity. With Fivetran, customer data arrives in their warehouses, canonical and ready to query, with no engineering or maintenance required. We’re proud that more organizations continue to leverage our technology every day to become truly data-driven.

About the Role

Fivetran is building data pipelines to power the modern data stack for thousands of companies.

As a Revenue Insights Manager, APAC at Fivetran, you will play a pivotal role in driving our company's revenue growth by providing data-driven insights to support our Asia Pacific (APAC) sales strategy. You will focus on analyzing sales data, identifying trends, and developing actionable recommendations to optimize sales efforts and improve customer acquisition and retention. Together with the APAC revenue leaders, you will design the roadmap for the rapidly growing APAC team to achieve their revenue growth targets.

This is a high visibility role within the organization with an incredible opportunity to drive impact and value quickly. The ideal candidate will be comfortable working in a fast-paced environment and adapting to changing priorities.

This is a full-time position based out of our Sydney office. Our hybrid work model offers a blend of remote flexibility and in-person collaboration, including two days in the office each week to connect and build as a team.

What You’ll Do

Serve as the business and thought partner to the Managing Director of APAC to design and execute on the GTM strategy, adjusting course as needed in service of hitting our targets consistently and efficiently.
Proactively identify business challenges and opportunities through data analysis, stakeholder interviews and process reviews, and devise practical, creative solutions to address them.
Roll up your sleeves to execute those solutions and measure the impact.
Lead regional funnel and forecast calls to ensure accountability and identify trends and any gaps in our GTM motion. Work cross-functionally to make our successes the norm and to close any gaps. 
Communicate and partner collaboratively with all levels within the organization to help drive revenue operations initiatives. 
Prioritize multiple high visibility/impact initiatives across multiple stakeholders to ensure we are meeting/exceeding our objectives. 
Partner with Analytics, Revenue Operations, GTM Enablement, Legal, Product, and Finance leaders to ensure the APAC business has the resources needed to hit their revenue targets.

Skills We’re Looking For

5+ years of experience in sales/revenue operations/strategy at a B2B SaaS company, including 2+ years supporting Enterprise Sales in APAC; Experience at a consumption based company is a plus.
Business acumen (how KPIs and health metrics interact, how to influence them) and data literacy (comfortable pulling data and building models to derive insights)
Comfortable with A/B testing and measuring the success of experiments
You think like an entrepreneur, demonstrate incredible ownership/initiative, and have a track record of making data-driven decisions and leading multiple concurrent cross-functional initiatives from inception to successful and timely completion.
Strategic mindset and problem-solving skills; ability to structure ambiguous problems and balance creativity and practicality when solutioning
You gain the trust of key stakeholders by actively listening and seeking to understand their views and needs. Consistently demonstrates respect and appreciation for others by empathizing, valuing their time and contributions, being available and responsive to their needs. Equally comfortable interacting with all levels within the organization, particularly Commercial, Enterprise, and Product Senior Leaders. 
You have experience with the levers of sales/customer success/alliances/marketing operations and with systems/tools including Salesforce, and Looker/Tableau. SQL knowledge is a plus.

RSU stock grants*

Company virtual happy hours, free food, and fun team-building activities
Monthly cell phone stipend
Access to an innovative mental health support platform that offers personalized care and resources in areas such as: therapy, coaching, and self-guided mindfulness exercises for all covered employees and their covered dependents.

Ad Text:
The role:

The Principal Benefits Analyst role supports the Manager, Benefits Management to provide a dedicated benefits and value management service to a complex ICT program, governed, managed and reported within the Delivery Services Branch, eHealth Queensland. It will review existing business cases and benefits information. This role will review and finalise benefit artefacts to organisational standards, facilitate agreement with stakeholders, maintain the benefit register and proactively track and report their progress and realisation.

Is this opportunity for you?

To be successful in this role, you must have:

Demonstrated experience leading and managing change management and benefits management activities within IM/ICT clinical programs and projects, preferably within the public healthcare industry.
Applied knowledge of APMG-International's Managing Benefits methodology at a practitioner level, and experience to identify, quantify, analyse, plan, track, realise, and optimise benefits.
Demonstrated stakeholder engagement and change management experience working effectively in multi-disciplinary including clinical teams, building productive relationships with team members and stakeholders, and mentoring program and project managers within the Delivery Services Branch.
Proven skills in consultation, facilitation, and both written and oral communication, tailored for use in a complex ICT project environment.

Flexible work arrangements are available and will be confirmed with the successful applicant. 

About eHealth Queensland:

eHealth Queensland is one of the largest Information and Communication Technology operations in the state. It is responsible for ensuring the smooth operation of information systems and technologies so that Queensland Health employees and health providers have access to information that supports the delivery of health care.

Benefits of working with eHealth Queensland:

By joining eHealth Queensland, you will work within a fast-paced, challenging and supportive environment where every employee plays a critical role in our ongoing success. 

You will enjoy a variety of benefits, including:

Competitive salary + generous superannuation and leave loading
Flexible working arrangements
Diverse work culture
Career training and development

To apply for this exciting opportunity, please provide the following information in your application:

Your current CV or resume
A short statement (maximum 2 pages) on how your experience, abilities, knowledge, and personal qualities are relevant for the role, taking into account the key responsibilities and attributes noted in the ‘How you will be assessed?' section of the Role Description.
